name: Google Artifact Registry (Python)
cost: Free tier of 0.5 GB storage per billing account; then $0.10/GB-month storage. Network egress is free within the same location/continent, ~$0.01/GB US/Canada inter-region, ~$0.02/GB intra-Europe, ~$0.05/GB intra-Asia, ~$0.08/GB inter-continent (non-Oceania), up to ~$0.15/GB to/from Oceania; outbound internet egress at Premium-tier rates. No per-seat/per-user fee.
language: Closed-source; implementation language not disclosed by Google
perf_notes: Fully managed; Google does not publish throughput/latency/memory benchmarks. Remote (proxy) repositories cache upstream artifacts on first request and serve cached copies thereafter; Python index files in remote repos are refreshed on a ~1 hour interval. Cleanup-policy background jobs apply changes within roughly one day.
scale_notes: Regional and multi-region repository locations; HA and scaling are handled transparently by Google's managed infrastructure (no nodes to operate). Covered by the Artifact Registry SLA. Per-billing-account storage is metered and effectively unbounded (pay per GB).
summary: Google Cloud's fully managed, IAM-secured private Python (and multi-format) package registry serving the PEP 503 simple API with twine upload, pip/uv install, and on-demand PyPI proxy/virtual repositories.
extras: Multi-format registry: same service hosts Docker/OCI, Maven, npm, Go, Apt, Yum, KubeFlow and generic artifacts alongside Python
Three repository modes: standard (host private), remote (on-demand PyPI/custom-upstream proxy/cache), and virtual (priority-ordered merge of upstreams)
Dependency-confusion mitigation by giving a private standard repo higher priority than the PyPI proxy inside a virtual repo and pointing clients at the virtual repo only

Authentication via Google IAM: short-lived OAuth access tokens (oauth2accesstoken), service-account JSON keys, and the keyrings.google-artifactregistry-auth keyring backend using Application Default Credentials

Workload Identity Federation lets CI (e.g. GitHub Actions OIDC) exchange external OIDC tokens for GCP credentials to publish, but this is platform-level keyless auth, not PyPI-style trusted publishing / PEP 740 (hence trusted_publishing_oidc=partial)
Cleanup policies (max 10/repo) with keep and delete rules by tag/version/package prefix and age; keep policies win over delete
Re-uploading an existing Python version is rejected (HTTP 400 'already exists'), giving overwrite protection
Cloud Audit Logs (Admin Activity always on; Data Access opt-in) under service artifactregistry.googleapis.com; logs/metrics flow to Cloud Logging and Cloud Monitoring

Artifact Analysis can scan Python dependencies only when they are inside container images pushed to AR, not for standalone wheels/sdists in a Python repository; SBOM (SPDX) generation is likewise container-image scoped
Infrastructure-as-code support via gcloud, REST/gRPC API, and Terraform google_artifact_registry_repository
VPC Service Controls and CMEK (customer-managed encryption keys) supported for the service
Python repository serves the canonical PEP 503 HTML simple API; no documented JSON simple API (PEP 691), PEP 700 extras, PEP 658/714 .metadata serving, or PEP 740 attestations
